dependencies: Largest single supplier represents 8% of total materials purchases
Reliance on commodity wood products subject to price fluctuations
Dependence on availability of qualified tradesmen
Supply chain constraints and cyclical fluctuations in commodity prices
risks: Product shortages and loss of key suppliers
Dependence on third-party suppliers and manufacturers
Inability of suppliers to meet supply needs in timely manner or quality standards
Delays in delivery of lumber and other products
Short-term changes in cost and/or availability of materials
Concentration risk - largest supplier is 8% of purchases

regulatory_risks: Federal, state, local and other regulations could impose substantial costs and/or restrictions on operations
Department of Transportation regulations applicable to fleet of delivery trucks
Work safety regulations promulgated by Department of Labor's Occupational Safety and Health Administration
Employment regulations, including immigration and work-authorization laws
Regulations promulgated by United States Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
Tariff regulations on imported products promulgated by Federal government
Accounting standards issued by FASB or similar entities
State and local regulations relating to escrow business
State and local zoning restrictions and building codes
Environmental laws, ordinances and regulations (federal, state, local)
Zoning, building design and safety, construction, energy and water conservation regulations
Regulatory restrictions may increase operating expenses and limit availability of suitable building lots
Changes to global trade policies may adversely impact business
Future changes to tax laws and regulations could have adverse impact
Liability for investigation or remediation of contamination on properties
More stringent standards regarding existing residual contamination
Changes in legislation, laws, rules or regulations
